reviews.review: Good game, I think the main story of the three paths could have been denser, especially the main one about the Pokémon League. The post-game story and the DLCs are good, I think they could have gone even deeper into the topic of paradoxes, like including them more in the main story, not just in the **** addition, they could have “locked” some areas so that it wouldn't be so easy to break the game. Something I'd like to see in the future, if they decide to keep the world open, is for trainers and gym leaders to have flexible teams based on your medals. That way, you could almost decide your path, with certain limitations, of course, such as giving you the freedom to explore the first four areas and go in whatever order you want for the first four gyms, and so on.Finally, improve the graphics. I'm not asking to be able to see the characters' sweat, but I would like to see better-constructed landscapes, with little or no popping. and something they absolutely must focus on in future games is that areas with wild Pokémon should feel more alive, not just with Pokémon walking or flying around, but doing different things, just like in the anime, with Pokémon interacting with the environment, and each Pokémon with its own nature changing depending on your behavior, as well as other trainers. It would be interesting to go along the routes and see NPCs trying to catch a Pokémon or training.

I believe that this game has better gameplay than sword and shield, but that sword and shield has the superior story. Both, however, pale in comparison to Sun and moon.
I think these games get a bad rep from modern consumers being overly critical and negative without playing the game to enjoy, but to rage at it. The current user score is a good example of people not having reigns over their emotions. P.S. Nemona is a freaking joke

This game is an embarrassment for a franchise that once defined handheld RPGs. On the Nintendo Switch — a console that has delivered technical marvels like Breath of the Wild and Super Mario Odyssey — this game looks and runs like a rushed beta.Performance: Constant frame drops, glitches, and visual bugs plague the experience. The open world is riddled with pop-in, broken textures, and laughably poor animations. It feels unfinished, as if Game Freak had no idea how to develop for modern hardware.Graphics: Bland environments, flat lighting, and character models that look outdated even by 3DS standards. The Switch has proven it can handle vast, beautiful worlds, but Scarlet settles for mediocrity.Gameplay: While the open-world concept had potential, it’s wasted on repetitive objectives, shallow exploration, and a lack of meaningful progression. The “freedom” is an illusion — the game funnels you into predictable battles and fetch quests.Story & Characters: Forgettable, uninspired, and padded with dialogue that adds nothing. Rivals and NPCs are caricatures, offering no emotional weight or memorable arcs.Overall Polish: Scarlet feels like a cash grab, banking on the Pokémon brand rather than delivering a product worthy of the Switch era.Verdict: Pokémon Scarlet is the worst kind of disappointment — a flagship title that fails to meet even basic standards of quality. On a console capable of breathtaking experiences, Scarlet stands out as a shameful reminder of how far the series has fallen.

It is decent if you play it on Switch 2, if you don't, it's a disgrace. The story is very fun, the graphics are bad but at least colorful, having an open world pokemon game is great but the world is also kind of empty except for the creatures.
Thanks to the Switch 2, it's now perfectly playable, now you can enjoy how bad it is at 60 FPS. And even then, it doesn't run perfectly in Area 0. What a terrible game.

I always thought I loved the Pokémon franchise, but Pokémon Scarlet showed me that wasn't quite the case. The game starts out interesting, the first steps give the impression that it's going to be something special. But when you compare the "open world" with Pokémon Legends: Arceus, it becomes clear that Scarlet is worse, even being the most **** addition, the game has many bugs and technical problems. Even after patches to fix them, almost nothing was resolved. The biggest problem, however, is the player's freedom. You can explore, but you are not significantly rewarded, which makes the experience **** the end, it seems that the franchise learned nothing from The Legend of Zelda, which showed how to give freedom and reward each choice. Scarlet starts well, but quickly gets lost in technical flaws and design **** one point, I just wanted this suffering to end.
ABSOLUTE PEAK; WHAT IS THAT ZELDA GAME. THIS IS THE MASTERPIECE EVERYONE SHOULD TALK ABOUT
Just when you thought Gamefreak couldn't make a cheaper game than Sword and Shield, they do.

A very hollow and grotesque experience. An amorphous collection of bile masquerading as a piece of entertainment software. It is as if a misshapen lump of digital flesh gained the ability to hate. Despite its clear disdain for the mortal coil along with everything good and holy in this world, it still struggles along, twisting and pulsating to maintain circulation and erratically convulsing past any brief respite of respiratory arrest. It has no mouth yet it must scream.

The technical side is completely outdated—it honestly feels like playing a PS2 game. The level design is awful, with a huge but empty and confusing map. Dungeons are basically nonexistent, just simple corridors. Pokémon are ridiculously small, so you spend more time watching your feet than exploring the world, which you’ll never really remember anyway. The Sierra Napada area is terrible, a real insult to players. Cities are totally empty, and NPCs have nothing interesting to say. The camera control is horrible. Most battles end up with the camera clipping through the ground, walls, or simply missing half of the Pokémon on screen. The lack of voice acting is becoming more and more painful, especially during key story moments. The idea of having three main questlines is nice, but it’s ruined by the lack of automatic level scaling. You can easily end up fighting a gym leader with level 20 Pokémon when your team is already level 50. Gym challenges are extremely dumb—the Psychic-type gym is probably the worst example. Characters are cute and well designed, but there are no side quests or events to interact with them, so you never get attached to anyone like in older games. The teachers are a bit more present since you attend classes and can talk to them between lessons—my favorite is Guaro. Pokémon models look bad; you can literally see the polygons. The new Terastalization mechanic is awful, especially compared to old systems like Gigamax or Mega Evolutions. The game is way too easy and lacks any difficulty settings. The idea of giving you the legendary Pokémon at the start is nice, but it’s not enough to save the experience. The post-game is almost nonexistent—basically locked behind paid DLC. In short: a game with some good ideas, but poorly executed in every way.

Okay, I have to be honest. I'm sending my copy of Pokemon Scarlet and Violet back. As a long-time fan, it's a real ****'s talk about the technical stuff first because you can't ignore it. This game is rough. I'm not just talking about the graphics looking a bit dated, I'm talking about the game slowing down constantly in the open world, especially around the lake. I had Pokemon and trees pop into existence right in front of me all the time. And the bugs... I once got stuck inside a rock and had to fast travel out. Another time, the game just froze during a picnic. It feels less like I'm playing a finished game and more like I'm testing a broken early **** the world itself feels so empty and lazy. Take the big main city, Mesagoza. It's huge, but it's all for show. You can't go into almost any of the buildings. Every street looks the same. Compare that to a place like Goldenrod City from the Gold and Silver days, which felt alive with a department store, a radio tower, the Magnet Train station. Mesagoza is just a pretty backdrop, and a repetitive one at that. The open world is just a bunch of empty fields. Where's the clever level design like the twisty paths of Mt. Coronet from Diamond and Pearl?But what really gets me is how the stories are told. The game has some serious moments, especially with Arven and his sick dog, Mabosstiff. But having these emotional scenes play out with just text boxes and characters staring blankly at each other is just awkward. It completely kills the mood. Games from the Nintendo DS era like Pokemon Black and White had way more compelling stories and characters without needing voice acting, but Scarlet and Violet's dialogue feels flat and forgettable in **** just feels like the developers cut every corner they could. For the most successful media franchise on the planet, this is not good enough. We've seen them make beautiful, polished games with heart on much weaker consoles. Playing this just makes me feel like they don't think we deserve better.

I have a lot of problems with this game, and I believe most of it starts with throwing away the things that sword and shield did right. The art style went from anime style that fit Pokémon to a dull color palette and uncanny semirealism that makes the protagonists look like porcelain dolls. The customization nose dived going from fun outfits with cute skirts, hats, heart pupils and vibrant hair colors that let me express the fairy type trainer I am, to now suddenly losing my individuality with a schooluniform and dozens of helmets. The world also suffers. Most of would is just empty grass lands with nothing noteworthy. No magical mushroom forests, volcanic caverns, desert dunes, ruins, reefs, large rivers. There is also severe Lack of environmental features like bushes, boulders; flowers, tall grass, trees and everything else. Most of the buildings you can’t interact with and half the ones you can you can’t enter and pull up menus instead. Lastly I find team stars story to drag the game down. The message is preachy with unsatisfying conclusion. It doesn’t help that this was the third playground bully team in row. 

A Pokémon game is about going on an adventure and most the aspects that immerse the player were neglected and team star as antagonist did nothing to push our imagination.

Unlimited exp candies is a ridiculously stupid thing to add, it just makes the game way too easy if you dont want to work. Don't even get me started on how bad the textures look and how poorly the game runs. New pokemon designs are uninspired. It used to take TPC a month to design each pokemon, now they just throw slop at a wall and don't even wait to see what sticks. What a disappointment.

Pokemon Yellow Version taught me how to read when I was 4 years old. Since then, I've played and beaten every single mainline game in the franchise, even going as far as completing the pokedex in many of them. That being said, Pokemon's attempt at an Open World adventure was such a massive letdown that I wonder if they were even trying at all. Pokemon Scarlet's open world lacks a genuine effort at world design or world building. There's a complete lack of environmental storytelling and nothing makes this more obvious than the fact that you can't go inside almost any of the buildings across the map. Meaningful NPC interactions are absent almost entirely. Open world games should reward exploration, something that this game fails tremendously at considering the open world is almost entirely empty. There are few-if-any dynamic events out in the world, which makes it feel bland and dead. The game's performance from a technical perspective was embarrassing at launch and is only remedied by purchasing the Switch 2, an entirely new console. The artwork from a visual perspective was heavily lacking. The game attempted a "realism" approach which made the pokemon look okay compared to Sword and Shield, but the rest of the world still looks awful. There's a clear lack of identity in the game's aesthetic -- it looks like almost every other 3D open world RPG on a mobile device, which is a stark contrast from games like BotW which have a unique aesthetic that works well on the Switch. The narrative direction was weak -- the Gym challenges were average at best but at least they were familiar. The Herba Mystica quest line was solid but the Team Star quest line was embarrassingly shallow and unrewarding. The story shines in Area Zero, but the fact that this is the end of the game means most people will have put it down way before then. The only things giving this game any points at all are the sound design, some decent new pokemon designs, and the Area Zero story line. Pokemon is the highest-grossing media franchise in the world... How they could have any dignity after releasing this slop is beyond me. In fact, the Switch as a console generation was tremendously embarrassing for Pokemon, so let's hope they can pick up the pieces before it's too late.

I hope they never do an open-world Pokémon game again unless it's like the Legends games, where it's good.

Pokemon games are very hard to rate because they've never really added anything super major or insanely exciting with each iteration. And they haven't really been the best games from an objective standpoint for a long time now. But, they are still, somehow, probably the best monster tamer games on the market. It's really surprising and kind of sad that all the other ones are so mediocre they can't even come close to a series that keeps rehashing the same game over and over again. But that's the sad world us monster tamer fans must live in.

If there was any monster tamers out there that remotely came close to Pokemon, Scarlet/Violet would be an easy 5. But since there's not, you kinda gotta give it a 7. Just because if you want a monster tamer, it's really the only game in town. Even now in 2025 and likely for the foreseeable future unless some studio shocks us and makes a Pokemon killer one day. 

But as for Scarlet/Violet, sandwiches are cool. I wish Pokemon were harder to catch but with the number of Pokemon now hitting way over 1,000, it's fully understandable why they're becoming easier and easier to catch. Because I put 150 hours into Violet and I only have 1/3 of all Pokemon. Also, Wonder Trade and Hacked Pokemon is what killed anything besides collecting Pokemon in the mainline Pokemon games. You can't trade with literally anyone online except real life friends who you know for a fact didn't hack pokemon or trade with strangers online. And battling is a complete joke as well as online trading because they're both filled with nothing but computer generated fake pokemon. So the only thing worth doing in mainline Pokemon games is slowly trying to catch em' all by yourself like I do or with trusted friends who you know don't trade online, hack or cheat. 

SCORE: 7 (Good) Only because there's literally no other monster tamers on the market worth playing.

Overall, this is a huge disrespect to the fanbase.

Big cash grab. Graphics are very bad and even using the new OLED model, many frame drops and lag depending on the region, so VERY BADLY optimized game. Story wise it is barely okay, but it is the worst story pokemon ever did. Most characters are annoying and most cutscenes are just plainly useless. controls are very basic, the removal of pokemon annimations (wich let's be honnest, are the worst since probably red and blue, and even then it had his charm). The end game is plainly horrible. Not beeing able to breed Paradox pokemons and needing to catch or hyper train them to get the best IV is annoying and time consumming. The tera raids are a good idea, but the execution is plainly horrible and lazy; First of the fact it is 1v4 and depend on other trainers to get by is very bad. Secondly, the many animations eating at your timer to kill the boss. Third is the whole timer mechanic making you use THE SAME BESTS POKEMONS OVER AND OVER, tanks are useless since boss cleanse themselves often, the barrier needs tera damage input to break it. Having a useless and VERY time consumming 4v1 boss raid mechanic is plainly a chore (again. Not looking for another job, already got one). And having ONLY THIS as end game PVE is an abomination; no battle tower, no 2v2 endless battles to try out comps. Just VERY TIME CONSUMING AND ANIMATION HEAVY RAIDS WICH ALSO DEPENDS ON YOUR OTHER COMPANIONS TO MANAGE TO COMPLETE. I found Sw/Sh was very shameful and boring, Scarlet/Violet is even worst. HeartGold and SoulSilver should be the kinds of love each pokemon game get

If the graphics were actually graphics and the game actually performed properly this would be a top 3 pokemon game, but it gets to sit nice and comfortably in their bottom 3 games along with BDSP and XY

Pokemon Scarlet embodies every mistake that an open world game can make. The world is technically open from the start, but freedom to explore does little when the world is empty and there is not much to do, no interesting side quest, no proper interactive NPCs, no interesting landmarks to explore. During the game you hear about cool places that the region have, but when arriving to those places you surely end up being disappointed due the ugly and uninspiring reality. Similarly towns you arrive during the game all feel unoriginal without any specific identity to them. All buildings are copy pasted and NPCs don´t have any interesting dialogue making towns feel really lifeless. In all honestly the game lacks any incentive to go beyond the set path. For the biggest region of the series Paldea feels really small and uninteresting.

Not to mention the lack of difficulty scaling for challenges. First time in the series you have a possibility to combat gyms in any order, which sounds good on paper, but outside specific challenges, player is incentivized to go specific path or end up being extremely over-or underleveled. The game doesn´t give any hints on the difficulty of each challenge either making anyone wanting to go in optimal order to rely on external information. The game being really easy doesn´t help either. While no one expects Pokemon to be difficult, Scarlet takes it one step further. If the player does anything extra between challenges like catching pokemon, battling trainers or exploring the region they inevitably end up being easily overleveled making the fighting really boring, again disincentivizing exploration if wanting any form of challenge.

All in all, Pokemon Scarlet feel like a linear Pokemon game with tech demo open world plastered to it in the late stages of development. It misses every point which makes an open world interesting and honestly would make much better game were it truly made to be linear without the illusion of freedom the half baked open world offers. 

For such an empty world it would at least be expected for it to work properly, but the performance is honestly atrocious and having stable 30fps is a rare occurrence. That could make sense if the game would have a lot going for it, but graphical fidelity and the art style being mediocre at best and draw distance being really bad don´t give any excuses for why it cannot perform at least decently.

Only saving grace for the game is the final area and the story contained there but you have to suffer through the rest of the game making it not really worth the wait. Otherwise the gameplay is exactly as expected from previous games and while not horrible itself, it does little to innovate the stale formula. Over all Pokemon Scarlet is a husk of an open world with no reason of exploration having implemented all of the problems of previous instalments and criticisms of open world formula without offering any solutions to them. As a long time Pokemon fan I am extremely disappointed especially after the previous game; Pokemon Legends Arceus that showed a lot of potential.
So much bugs and glitches. It's 2025 and some bugs and glitches still haven't fixed
I honestly don't know why everyone hates this game. I have more than 150 hours in that game and I have found zero glitches, and all the rivals are really good.

An open world main entry Pokemon game was always the dream when I was younger, so I do love Pokemon Scarlet. It is buggy though and in handheld the game doesn’t run that smoothly! Open world means that you can tackle the gym leaders in any order, though that’s not recommended as they don’t scale based on your Pokémon’s level so there is still an order that needs to be followed. A promising start to the open world style of Pokemon which will surely be refined when the Switch 2 is released.
While the game is laggy and a bit glitchy, it is a decent game, and if it weren’t as laggy, it would probably be a 10, Paldea is a great region with many cool trainers, I do recommend.
It Is A Good Quality Game But Graphic Issues Make Game Worse Although Main Story Is Good
Best pokemon game in ages, everything about this other than the performance issues and the ongoing issue from the last gen of not having a national dex is fantastic.

The gameplay was great, the open world was fun, the characters and the pokemon look great, this game had the best story since black and white,has decent difficulty. I really loved the climax with area 0 which looked great, the pokemon and npcs look amazing and got updated textures. But the games visuals are awful ,there are a ton of bugs, there is no level scaling.  This game is a 6/10 for me.

Huge waste of money this game is clearly a cash grab . A pokemon that looks like a motorcycle? I refuse to call that a pokemon I sit here and play my fire red / pokemon platinum thinking how great these games are it is just sickening how pokemon is so focused on the money now they forget they had something good already going and to change/ruin everything everyone loves is wrong.  I could go on but I'll never be heard . Horrible game everything about it is bad .

Oh boy, I sure hope it's not a broken bug infested disaster that everybody gives a free pass to because it's Pokemon! ...oh, it is? Damn that's unfortunate. If anybody rates this above a 5 I genuinely question if I played the same game as them. Absolutely abysmal that wouldn't pass as a $20 indie game on Steam.
Stories were kind of meh, gameplay gets pretty repetitive, and the game itself was all kinds of buggy.

The game has a really incredible story outcome, however the 3 stories are really boring and repetitive, they feel very flat and without charisma. The whole map feels like cardboard, the cities do not seem to have life, none of the structures that there are visitable, they are only "png" with collision without forgetting the terrible performance of the game which has constant performance drops and a disastrous visual section. The fights do not have considerably good animations compared to the previous installments of pokemon making the game look like an "uncompleted beta"

Pretty fun but lacks a lot polish. The game is really easy and you can get over levels easily. You can meet pokemon inside of walls sometimes and the camera can be really annoying. Atleast the gameplay is fun

Good mid/end of story and... Thats all. It is not normal that the camera moves so badly, has so many visual bugs and causes nausea, I am not saying this in a derogatory way, the motion sickness caused by this game is terrible. Except Toby Fox Songs and one song extra, the soundtrack is soulless like all the open world. Pokemon was dying years ago, but now its worst.

There is no excuses for this!

I can tolerate some unfinished things in Pokémon games, like DP slow, XY lacking of conclusion or the mom character of SM never made up the boxes in the house. 

But this is too far!

While the sales are good, it's not hide the true problem, the game is completely unfinished and unpolished, it's a shame that this is acceptable for the community and consumers in general.

Ignore the bugs, frame drops, and glitches. The world is empty, 90% of buildings cannot be entered, towns are barren, pickup items are meaningless, copy and pasted food shops are meaningless and the food items and their mixed usage too complicated that I didn't bother using any through my playthrough. Why are there 3 rng boosts for each meal, I'm not trying to google and min/max items or just buy something for the sake of one stat and not utilize the others.

This is an "open world" game where you choose your treasure hunt, but stop as soon as you deviate from the intended path the entire game falls apart. If you don't follow the hollow intended path be prepared to be wildly overleveled without doing any autobattles.

I only engaged in pokemon fights when I saw a pokemon I hadn't caught yet, sometimes I'd KO by mistake but even this being the case I was consistently very overleveled for tasks in the game. You cannot choose your own adventure, you cannot ignore a certain part of the game say team star then return to it later with proper scaling and get any value out of it. The game makes the critical mistake of allowing you to enter kitakami right from the beginning which if you choose to pursue will destroy any balancing in the game. The game will sometimes surprise you with impossible battles such as the ogre clan and you just have to suffer slowly losing all your pokemon till you run out because the game has no sort of balancing what so ever.

By far the worst pokemon experience I've ever had and close to one of the worse game experiences I've ever had.

I like that tall grass is gone and you choose your battles, the graphics are ok infact character assets generally look pretty good it's the copy and paste assets like trees, buildings, and grass that they decided to skimp on maybe for the sake of performance but none the less. Dynamic battle engagement is cool but so often clips the camera into or under the environment before/after battles.

The biggest issue is the progression and lack of scaling, especially for a game that tauts itself as you being able to go on your own "treasure hunt" (their words) and yet you are punished severely for deviating and it can be game ruining. 

The whole experience feels generally empty, meaningless, and not an adventure. I miss nurse joy and the pokemart too, they don't give the "gas stops" any character, they feel empty and meaningless like the rest of this game. I miss entering someone's home and talking bout what's on TV, their favorite pokemon, trading pokemon, hearing town news, etc. Why do they have buildings if no one is ever in them?

I can only recommend this game to people who are able to tolerate constant frame drops, technical issues, and can make their own fun.

The best moments of scarlet and violet come towards the end. The games have awful performance and issues that make them unenjoyable until the story picks up enough momentum to be interesting. The open world itself is only useful for encountering wild pokemon but the encounters were handled better in Legends Arceus by the active capture system. 

Buy this if you like playing online as the Terastal mechanic is extremely fun to use, otherwise avoid it as the single player is extremely extremely disappointing.
The lowest point of Pokémon games. It lags, it bugs, graphic is even worse than Arceus (far worse). It'sa pity because story could have been a good 8. Too broke to consider good points.
The worst experience in games of all my life. I want my money back and i never will buy a Pokémon game again. I lot of bugs.
Is not a terrible game, but there are problems like bugs, glitches and low frame rate, i hope that Game Freak learned from this.

While the performance issues are very hard to forgive, they are far from the biggest detractor for the ninth generation of Pokémon. As a matter of fact, I propose a very strange idea. The biggest detractor of this game is every other Pokémon game on the Switch.

Every aspect of this game is a step back. On its own merit, it would be good. Not great, not excellent, but good. However, with a world that feels emptier than Legends: Arceus, raids that feel worse than those in Sword/Shield, and a story that has been completely disjointed (as compared to any of the previous generations) due to a lack of controlled pacing both in terms of gameplay and writing, there is nothing here that doesn't feel worse than something. Given that the core fanbase consists of longtime players, this comparison is unavoidable, even on a massive scale.

That said, there is merit to the game. A cast of new mons that, personally, I enjoy, and methods of shiny hunting being more accessible than ever means that this game hardly felt like a slog for many hours. However, it is undeniable that the quality has slipped, and for any new fans, I will definitely continue to direct them to the previous generation.

Pokemon Scarlet and Violet had the potential to be the best mainline Pokemon games in the entire franchise thus far. However, due to extremely poor performances issues, a rushed and unpolished world, as well as the lack of general care of the state the game was released in, they end up currently being the worst the series has to offer. 

Don't get me wrong, I love Pokemon! They did improve upon a lot with these games. For example, the Tera phenomenon was very interesting to play with, added new elements to the game, and really shook things up for both Casual and Competitive players. Having an open world, as well as three different stories was also a great addition as it deviates from the traditional Pokemon formula we have had for the past 20 years. Unfortunately, due to the lack of time being put into the game, the open world ends up still being linear since the lack of level scaling makes you play the game in order or ascending levels, instead of being able to truly choose your own adventure.

As of writing this, the game has yet to have had a meaningful patch fixing any of these issues, so more than likely the game will continue to exist in the awful state it has been in since launch. It is quite frankly unacceptable for The Pokemon Company to release a game in this state, as they the highest-grossing media franchise to exist in the entire world, and definitely have the financial resources to create higher quality games then they have here.

I personally will not be supporting The Pokemon Company's games anymore until the they begin to release them in a finished state, and would advise others who truly care about the health of current and future Pokemon games to do the same. Thank you.

I played Pokemon Scarlet and I have to say, that I liked many things in there. The new functions like the portable boxes and the new Pokemon Centers maked the gameplay faster and stopped tieback and forth running, while training pokemon. Also I liked the storyline, even if the Characters were characterless and monotonous. But now to the negative points. The world feels really unfinished and empty. Often there are big empty areas with some trees or rocks. Nothing really special. Also the towns are boring, because you cant go in the houses anymore and most of the characters are unnecessary. No cool side quests, no little rewarding. Everything is pointless and unnecessary and I had less reason to explore the world than in previous parts of the series.I like the new way of an open world pokemon, but everything feels scripted and characterless. You can do better GameFreak!

Open world concept sure, it was interesting,otherwise, abysmal, bad dialogue, gym mini games that where nonsensical and idiotic, a rival that is beyond annoying, a pokemon academy that makes no sense and overall a dull and mediocre story. This game has nothing of the fun simple pokemon we grew up with.

Pokémon Scarlet and Violet is a mixed bag to say the least. While is had many fun ideas, interesting concepts, and possibly the best writing in the whole series, it’s impossible to ignore the train wreck that is this game visuals and performance. The game runs ok at best, and it barely runs at all at worst. And improvements made to textures are impossible to see with all the pixelation on display. (It’s also worth mentioning that the animations, were improved in some ways in this title, but are still a notable step back from Legends Arceus) I want to love Scarlet and Violet, but I can’t. Let’s hope that Game Freak can learn from this moving forward.
Fun to play but if you want a well-polished Pokémon game without bugs, don't buy this game, Honestly Over-hated though.

Some questionable creative decisions. The choice to commit to a fully open world map advanced the series but at the cost of performance. The graphics are pretty poor and then you get into the frame drops and other visual bugs. Some of the new pokedex entries are completely mailed in where others are going to be favourites for coming generations.

I really liked the storyline in this series. I know, I know, there are some strong technical issues, but I didn't experience them with the handheld switch. I honestly didn't mind the direction they went with this one and really do think the storyline was borderline cinematic.
The new pokemon are all so adorable and the the new features are great welcome to the series

I kinda went into this game knowing about things like it's performance issues, and to be honest, that's my only major downside with this (aside from the lackluster story, but that's an issue with most modern Pokemon Games, so I stopped caring about the story as much and started expecting lackluster stories). This game is genuinely the most fun I've ever had with a Pokemon game, and it's all because of the open world aspect of it. It feels so free, it feels like a real adventure, and the fact that there are rarely any hard roadblocks keeping you out of specific areas (as in unnatural ones, like a random herd of Psyduck blocking the way to a city) makes it feel that much more free. This also kind of helps with the issue of difficulty, but not really. Difficulty almost kept this from getting an 8, but I found that the lack of difficulty was kind of balanced out by how fun it was, so it barely made it. I didn't find a lot of unique strategies used by trainers, because the closest thing to a strategy someone would have is something to counter 1 super-effective type, which is more than some games have, but still... would've liked to have seen more use of held items. And I can't ignore the performance issues. The game can go below 20 fps sometimes, but mostly, it handles 30 to 40FPS well... that is, unless you go in a more crowded area. The crowded areas are so laggy, and I think it's a byproduct of the graphics, which are gorgeous, but gorgeous in the way a poisonous plant can be; yeah, it's pretty to look at... but once it does stuff, it ain't gonna be pretty, that's for sure. Sure, you can just play it on a stronger platform, but I don't know if Nintendo's next console will be backward compatible with the Switch, and if it is, I don't know how much more bearable it can make the lag, but still. I would've given this a 9... but the lackluster story already barred it from a 10, and the performance issues were major enough to knock it down to an 8. I also don't wanna count DLC into this review, but if I did, then this would be a 9. The DLC is so good, but we're talking about the main game. It's the most fun Pokemon game so far, but it's also the worst performing (as in how it runs), and probably the only game where I've ever wanted the graphics to be worse so that I don't have to panic about the game crashing whenever I enter an area with 8 or more breathing organisms. Performance issues aside, this game's fun. I would recommend if you can either handle lag, or if you know of a way to run the game on something stronger.

The game itself is really good! I love the open world aspect and the story and characters, for a Pokemon game, are incredible. However, it's hard to overlook the graphical issues and the lack of level scaling makes it really hard to enjoy the open world. This is a step in the right direction for the Pokemon franchise, but if they really want to tap into the open world thing they have a lot of things to improve on.

I cannot tell if im getting older or Pokemon games are just getting worse. These games are not only easy, but the quality of game that Nintendo can put out is pitifully bellow where it should be. This game made me sad, because as someone that has played just about every single pokemon game up until this point, this one I think finally made me quit. Unless I decide to come back to VGC, pokemon is dead to me... however a good game is all they need to release to get me to come back, but looking at their track record... I doubt that any time soon -_-

If the game hadn't been so unpolished, the score would have been a 9. The visuals are directly from the N64, and the flaws and glitches are really bothersome. But my assessment **** is dependent on how much fun and excitement I have while playing it. In my view, I prefer fun over the graphics. For that reason, I thought this game was close to a 9. It is most likely the Pokemon game with the most potential and was among the finest in terms of content. The game may have been a masterpiece if it had been more well-polished. Nintendo should rebuild this game, improving the graphics and, most importantly, addressing all the faults and errors. If they did that, it would probably be one of the best pokemon games of all time.

I'm a long time Pokemon fan. This Game is verry fun and I have more than 100 hours in this game. AlthoughI I had so much fun, the graffics are terrible and often I just got stuck in the air for 6 seconds. And I'm not even going to start talking about the frame rate.

Although the graphics are worse than the other Pokémon games on switch, they do improve lots of Pokémon models and animations. The story is the best we’ve seen and battles, especially late game can get fairly difficult. Frame rate wasn’t much of an issue for me. Open world is a bit empty and it feels like you have to constantly fly from one side of the map to the other to follow a logical level progression, which doesn’t feel right. But as usual, great new set of mons, amazing soundtrack, and fun gameplay.

I DON'T CARE! I will say it I don't care what people think. Compared to Pokémon Sword and Shield Pokémon Scarlet and Violet are top tier games! I'll say it here anyone who here has played pixelated Pokémon Yellow, Red, Blue, you lot have no right to complain. You have played pixelated low fps Pokémon before.

Pokémon Scarlet and Violet have some of the best Storytelling and best characters to date! I have played through both Scarlet and Violet multiple times and have encountered no big issues or glitches. Not only that but it's the only other game that I have actually completed the Pokedex for! I only put effort into the Pokedex if I truly enjoy a Pokémon game to the fullest!

I didn't complete the Alola dex, but that's because, one, version exclusive Pokémon, two, no one to trade with. The only other game that I completed the Pokedex in is Legends Arceus! So suffice to say this game has value. Pokémon Scarlet stands as the Pokémon game I have put the most hours into on the Nintendo Switch.

The characters are amazing and the stories are breathtaking and I say Pokémon Scarlet and Violet, as of writing this are the best Pokémon games on the Nintendo Switch!
The game is great and it makes fun the only 2 Things i have to say are it get's boring if you play to long and the graphics aren't the best but the music and everything Else is really good

( I played this game a bit after the actual release so my opinion is on the later versions). Pokémon scarlet/violet is generally a good game. It is definitely a great step into the right direction for future Pokémon games. Its open world is very expansive but has some flaws. When I was playing the game, I didn’t notice any major problems aside from some slight frame drops like in the large lake area in the north west . Even after completing the game and finishing the Pokédex, I didn’t have any visual or physical bugs aside from two small glitches, which didn’t affect my gameplay. While yes, the open world has flaws, I think it’s still amazing.  The story is nothing to scoff at as well, the characters are interesting and refreshing. The story keeps my attention and makes me actually want to see what happens next. I understand why it was released when it was, but I feel that Nintendo should just take their time to make games at full potential. I hope to see great things from the Nintendo company.

I rated this game an 8/10 because although the game is very fun and entertaining, there are still bugs in places. I didn’t get the game as soon as it came out, so I was not a part of the group of players who had to enjoy the game while it was virtually unplayable due to massive glitches, bugs, and extreme lag. Though even after many bug fixes, it does still lag a bit under certain conditions but not to the point where you cannot work around it. I have 200+ hours in the game after finishing it in its entirety and sometimes the lag in specific areas still annoys me but I have been thoroughly pleased by the outcome and the DLC only adds to that delight. Being only the second game I’ve owned of the franchise and coming after what I've heard be considered a groundbreaking approach to the game, I was still extremely satisfied.

Honestly I can see why lots of people hate this game, but at the same time I absolutely loved it, from start to finish, it was the first game that replayed more than once, it keeps you locked on the scree, it's the first game that made me want to complete the Pokéde, the 3 stories we're top 3 of the franchise, there were lots of technical issues but sometimesi didn't even felt them, the Music was awesome and some parts of the map(I don't particularly like it) are beautiful.
I hink haven't had a good experience witha Pokémon games since Black and White 2, this game made me fell in love again withthe franchise and even with all their faults I had an incredible experiencez there are som many things to do and now it's bug free, so you can play it really well, the only thing is the fps drops but it's not that big of a deal, I’ll always wish that Game Freak had taken another year or maybe even just a few more months to polish it enough to be playablefrom the start.
Despite graphical and perfromance issues (which was never the focus of the series) these games are the most fun I had with Pokemon in a long time.
Just horrible. Forced exp share, **** ps2 graphics, memory leak, bugs, no more set moves, no building interiors, bland and empy world. An utter embarrassment.

Straight up the best game dude, it was pretty bad in the start but now (2024) it is basically bug free and it is very good, and the dlc it is just top tier, even without the dlc you have a lot to do in it. It is not like what others said it is better waaaaaaay better

Would definitely recommend.

The open world was very fun and welcoming addition to the pokemon franchise! I hope they use it a lot more! I saw reviews saying it was "un playable" I bought it a week after release and despite the lag in the water it was very playable. Barely glitches and the glitches I did have were ones that didn't affect gameplay! The story was good, great characters, and overall a very fun game! I would recommend this to a lot of people!
An absolute mess ****. Everything too the piss poor performance and graphics to the entirely uninteresting open world makes you wish you were playing Legends: Arceus instead.

First 10 hours were amazing! I was shocked how diverse this game was at the beginning! I really felt the thrill of adventure... But that feeling quickly disappeared since I realized all of pokémon quests are just repetition from first quest... And it is really sad because this game could be really something! Generally speaking I had good time with this game but at the end I was so bored and frustrated because all of that dull repetitions and amazingly boring and stupid quest lines... I wish that they cold add skippable conversations because those are way to long! And why there isn't any voice acting? It's a solid game with lot of fun but with a lot of bugs, annoying things and I can't give more that 6 to such big company with such big budget.

I didn't play this on release, so a lot of the potential performance issues experienced were very minimal and more so limited to specific areas of the game. Other than that, I found myself enjoying this title far more than previous ones in many ways. The quality of life improvements from previous entries is significant for those who play more than the initial game. There is a very achievable and manageable endgame for both Competitive Pokemon, and Raids. (PVP and PVE.) The story was shockingly deep and well done, keep in mind this is a presentation for someone more teen-aged. It covers more complex topics that an audience that age could tackle with on a daily basis, while delivering the reliable "feel-good" moral message that Nintendo always does.

The new entries Pokemon wise are about average, there is roughly as many "cool" new ones as there are "uninspired" ones. But, to some extent that is expected as we're now beyond 1,000. 

A number of mechanical improvements, and some level of open world exploration is enjoyable and fun to navigate around. We are closely approaching a way to enjoy a true open world main entry for Pokemon. 

The DLC has been solid so far, about what you should expect for some extra content. 

Overall, it's a safe step forward. While we can agree that we want to see GameFreak be a bit more daring in the future, I cannot say that any parts of this game were particularly disappointing in any way. There are measurable improvements in many aspects of the game, from co-op, to gym progression, to story delivery, to end game PVP and PVE accessibility, and many more.

The one thing I would criticise, and it's more of a stylistic choice than actual mechanical issue is the new "Terastalize" mechanic. I just think it visually looks awful in comparison to Gigantamax or Mega Evolutions. Mechanically it's function is enjoyable and fresh, but visually it is just very unenjoyable.

Let's get the obvious out of the way first. The game is unfinished and buggier than an early game forest in Pokemon. That being said, this is not a bad game. It has high highs and very low lows. The performance is by far the worst part, so getting around that allows for the discovery of an actual game, one that could even be deemed as great. The story is unexpectedly phenomenal and gives generation five a run for its money. The characters are some of the best the series has seen, and the new Pokemon are pretty average overall. There are some really goods ones as well as some forgettable ones. Everything else stylistic of Pokemon like the music and dialogue remain strong. There are some phenomenal tracks, and the dialogue gives the game feeling and emotion, almost giving the impression that the developers cared about what they were producing. Overlooking the awful performance and glitches make Pokemon Scarlet a good game, but since no one can overlook those troublesome things, it deserves an average rating.

This game is not even half as bad as people say it is. Everything about this game is amazing except for the graphics and frame rate issues, and I've only ran into one major bug even though I've been playing the game since day 1. Storywise and gameplaywise, it's a masterpiece. The whole game is a mystery. And the end of the game is the most entertaining ending in a pokemon game. Pokemon designs are cool and clearly inspired, not necessarily "just designed to be plushes". The designs have a lot going for them. This game is way underrated. The only bad thing that you'll run into is frame rate issues. I have to admit, that was a huge problem. However, if you can look past these performance issues, you'll be able to enjoy the game and it's clever story for what it's worth. The characters are very relatable and have their own unique personalities too, which will probably make you more intrigued and interested to see what happens in the story. This game has the best writing of any pokemon game, and all of the characters and stories feel real.
I'm not sure what else to say, but I hope that you can look past it's performance issues and realize how engaging, interesting, and compelling the characters, story, and writing are when you try it for yourself. It's definitely worth the money, especially with all of its post-game content as well.

Let's start with the obvious: This game is unpolished. Framerate issues, graphics problems, bugs, you name it. But the issues don't stop there: there is no level scaling and the game quickly becomes to easy.
However, if you ignore the technical issues, go through the different gyms and bosses in ascending order, and try to only use Pokemon of the same level as the respective gyms, this is the best Pokemon game of all time.
The open world is great. The feeling of exploring the world, discovering items, new Pokemon and NPCs is amazing. Annoying random encounters have been removed. And there have been a bunch of quality of life changes that make the game significantly less grindy. The new Terastalizing mechanic makes battles even more interesting.
All in all, the core gameplay loop of catching Pokemon and building teams has never been as fun as in this game, especially if the player takes some steps to ensure that things go in the right order and are not too easy. There is something in the classic linear top-down games that gives them life and coziness that are not present here. But still, this is a very good Pokemon game!
I love it, but honestly, to much pressing (A) haha. Very much action and downloading DLC are bigger than expected.

From the corny story and game of Sword or Shield, this game takes a nicer route. Besides some FPS issues and small nuisance, this game is so much fun and doesn't deserve the review bombing it has gotten. It's actually awesome!

Great concept of story progress, love the fast fight sending your pokemon and the picnic function, BGM is great too, but it's difficult to put it higher than some previous pokemon spundtracks.
Only reason its not a 10 is because of some performance drops during the playthrough.
Other than that great game!

I have played since gen 1, and it's the most fun I've had in a Pokemon game. The story is amazing, and I love how the three stories converge, and it's done naturally. The music is also fantastic, my favourite being the final boss. It's not without it's flaws though, the most prominent being the frame rate drops and occasional glitches, although that didn't take away the fun I had playing this game, but I understand how it could be a deal breaker for some.

Overall it's a step in the right direction, and I hope Game Freak can fix the frame rate issues and glitches in future games, because if there weren't any performance issues, this would be close to a 10. Highly recommend if you're a fan of Pokemon, and if you don't mind occasional frame rate drops and glitches

The open world is a great step forward for the series the biggest issue is the buggyness of the game i would more say this is most likely due to the switch hardware and gamefreak needs to implement difficulty options the gyms and elite 4 and champion need to level based off the players level but this is the issue pokemon has always had but again the open world was a step in the right direction it was awesome to stumble upon the later gym early trying to battle a level 40ish with my level 20 team the series needs things like this to make it better there needs to be scaling if there is going to be an open world especially considering how easy it is to level your team now with the experience candys overall its pokemon the story is okay i did like the story with titan quest it was heart felt and kind of grounded a boy that loves his dog and would do anything to try and make him feel better

Absolutely love this game. It's a big step up for Gamefreak, which is what we all want to see and the gameplay and mechanics are perfect. Once they figure out a better graphics system, Pokemon games might just be perfect again.
